programme.sub-title: Nemanja Vidić grew up in war-torn Serbia and went on to become one of the greatest defenders in English football. PL Stories sits down with him to explore his journey from Belgrade to Manchester. We also hear from former coaches and players about how his childhood shaped him into the person he is today and his lasting impact on the Premier League.

programme.title: PL STORIES EP11: JARRED GILLETT - THROUGH THE EYES OF A REFEREE
programme.sub-title: Jarred Gillett made history earlier this year when he became the first Premier League referee ever to be fitted with a special head-mounted camera during Crystal Palace’s home victory over Manchester United. PL Stories is granted exclusive access to Gillet and his officiating team on matchday, offering fans a unique insight into refereeing at the highest level. Premier League match officials and in particular referees are often the most scrutinised individuals in the game and this show reveals how difficult a task they have.
programme.desc: Jarred Gillett made history earlier this year when he became the first Premier League referee ever to be fitted with a special head-mounted camera during Crystal Palace’s home victory over Manchester United. programme.title: THE BIG INTERVIEW EP09: MIKEL JOHN OBI
programme.sub-title: Having risen from the bustling streets of Nigeria to the pinnacle of professional football in England, Mikel John Obi’s story is a remarkable one that’s seen him win 2 Premier League titles, 4 FA Cups, 2 European trophies and 3 Africa Cup of Nations titles. This week on The Big Interview, the former Chelsea man opens up on the challenges of fame and fortune and lifts the lid on each of the managers he worked with at Chelsea.

programme.desc: In January 2023, Everton appointed Sean Dyche as manager, and gave him the critical task of keeping the club in the Premier League. We revisit the key moments of the Toffees' fight for survival, as told by the charismatic boss and his veteran captain Seamus Coleman.

programme.title: REVIEW OF THE SEASON 2023/2024 (MNC)
programme.sub-title: The 2023/24 Premier League season will be forever remembered as one of the greatest campaigns ever. From an absorbing title race, Jürgen Klopp leaving Liverpool to Luton living the dream in the top-flight – Review of the Season documents all of the thrilling stories to emerge across the 380 matches.
programme.desc: The 2023/24 Premier League season will be forever remembered as one of the greatest campaigns ever. From an absorbing title race, Jürgen Klopp leaving Liverpool to Luton living the dream in the top-flight – Review of the Season documents all of the thrilling stories to emerge across the 380 matches.

programme.title: PL STORIES EP15: TIM CAHILL
programme.sub-title: With over 200 appearances for Everton, Tim Cahill established himself as the high-energy midfielder who helped drive the side. For PL Stories this week, Cahill talks through his journey to the Premier League from battling his way to make it as a footballer in his native Australia, leaving home as a teenager and adapting to English life, scoring in a World Cup as well as the difficult decision to leave Goodison Park after 8 years.
programme.desc: With over 200 appearances for Everton, Tim Cahill established himself as the high-energy midfielder who helped drive the side. programme.title: PL STORIES EP10: IPSWICH TOWN
programme.sub-title: In the first senior manager’s job of his career, Kieran McKenna has achieved something nobody else has managed this decade: Winning back-to-back promotions from League One to the Premier League, in the process ending Ipswich’s 22-year absence from top flight football. We go behind the scenes with the team as they prepare for the opening game of the new season whilst also exploring the club’s unique history.

programme.title: THE BIG INTERVIEW EP09: MIKEL JOHN OBI
programme.sub-title: Having risen from the bustling streets of Nigeria to the pinnacle of professional football in England, Mikel John Obi’s story is a remarkable one that’s seen him win 2 Premier League titles, 4 FA Cups, 2 European trophies and 3 Africa Cup of Nations titles. This week on The Big Interview, the former Chelsea man opens up on the challenges of fame and fortune and lifts the lid on each of the managers he worked with at Chelsea.

programme.title: PL REWIND EP03: SEAN DYCHE - MY EVERTON STORY
programme.sub-title: In January 2023, Everton appointed Sean Dyche as manager, and gave him the critical task of keeping the club in the Premier League. We revisit the key moments of the Toffees' fight for survival, as told by the charismatic boss and his veteran captain Seamus Coleman.

programme.title: PL STORIES EP07: FERGIE v WENGER
programme.sub-title: Widely regarded as the two best managers in Premier League history, Sir Alex Ferguson and Arsene Wenger went toe to toe on the touchline on numerous occasions over the years. But what made their rivalry so fierce? We take a look at the intense clashes, memorable moments on and off the pitch and the monumental impact their duels had on English football's modern era.

programme.title: PL STORIES EP05: BLACKPOOL - THE IMPOSSIBLE DREAM
programme.sub-title: We talk to the cast of characters who helped Blackpool reach the dizzy heights of the Premier League in the 2010-11 season. programme.title: PL STORIES EP06: THE ENTERTAINERS
programme.sub-title: Nicknamed ‘The Entertainers’, Kevin Keegan’s Newcastle team of the 1995-96 season set the Premier League alight with their confidence and flair that took them agonisingly close to winning their first Premier League title. In this show, we speak to those that were part of that incredible journey and reflect on some of those unforgettable moments that captured hearts and forever changing the club's legacy.
programme.desc: Nicknamed ‘The Entertainers’, Kevin Keegan’s Newcastle team of the 1995-96 season set the Premier League alight with their confidence and flair that took them agonisingly close to winning their first Premier League title. programme.title: PL STORIES EP15: TIM CAHILL
programme.sub-title: With over 200 appearances for Everton, Tim Cahill established himself as the high-energy midfielder who helped drive the side. For PL Stories this week, Cahill talks through his journey to the Premier League from battling his way to make it as a footballer in his native Australia, leaving home as a teenager and adapting to English life, scoring in a World Cup as well as the difficult decision to leave Goodison Park after 8 years.
programme.desc: With over 200 appearances for Everton, Tim Cahill established himself as the high-energy midfielder who helped drive the side. programme.title: PL STORIES EP32: (Subbed) MANCHESTER CITY - THE SEASON THAT STARTED IT ALL
programme.sub-title: In a nail-biting climax to the 2011-2012 Premier League season, Manchester City clinched the title with unparalleled drama. Facing Queens Park Rangers on the final matchday, City secured a 3-2 victory with Sergio Agüero's iconic last-minute goal, securing their first league title in 44 years and etching that game into footballing folklore. They have since become the dominant power in English football and we look at how it all began.

programme.title: THE BIG INTERVIEW EP09: MIKEL JOHN OBI
programme.sub-title: Having risen from the bustling streets of Nigeria to the pinnacle of professional football in England, Mikel John Obi’s story is a remarkable one that’s seen him win 2 Premier League titles, 4 FA Cups, 2 European trophies and 3 Africa Cup of Nations titles. This week on The Big Interview, the former Chelsea man opens up on the challenges of fame and fortune and lifts the lid on each of the managers he worked with at Chelsea.
programme.desc: Having risen from the bustling streets of Nigeria to the pinnacle of professional football in England, Mikel John Obi’s story is a remarkable one that’s seen him win 2 Premier League titles, 4 FA Cups, 2 European trophies and 3 Africa Cup of Nations titles. programme.title: PL STORIES EP18: DYLAN TOMBIDES
programme.sub-title: He was a star of Australia's U23 and U17 teams and made his West Ham debut at just 18 years of age, but in 2011 Dylan Tombides life was turned upside down when he received a diagnosis of testicular cancer following a routine drug test during the Under-17 FIFA World Cup. Despite a courageous three-year battle, he tragically succumbed to the illness at the age of 20 in 2014. We look back on the life of Dylan told through those who knew and loved him best, and how 10 years on since his unfortunate passing his memory still lives on through the DT38 foundation.
programme.desc: He was a star of Australia's U23 and U17 teams and made his West Ham debut at just 18 years of age, but in 2011 Dylan Tombides life was turned upside down when he received a diagnosis of testicular cancer following a routine drug test during the Under-17 FIFA World Cup. programme.title: PL REWIND EP04: BOU v LUT 23/24
programme.sub-title: In December 2023, Bournemouth v Luton is abandoned when Tom Lockyer faced a life-threatening situation on the pitch. He survived and watched the rematch, which turned into a Premier League classic. Andoni Iraola, Lewis Cook and Antoine Semenyo tell the story.
programme.desc: In December 2023, Bournemouth v Luton is abandoned when Tom Lockyer faced a life-threatening situation on the pitch. He survived and watched the rematch, which turned into a Premier League classic. Andoni Iraola, Lewis Cook and Antoine Semenyo tell the story.

programme.title: PL REWIND EP03: SEAN DYCHE - MY EVERTON STORY
programme.sub-title: In January 2023, Everton appointed Sean Dyche as manager, and gave him the critical task of keeping the club in the Premier League. We revisit the key moments of the Toffees' fight for survival, as told by the charismatic boss and his veteran captain Seamus Coleman.
programme.desc: In January 2023, Everton appointed Sean Dyche as manager, and gave him the critical task of keeping the club in the Premier League. We revisit the key moments of the Toffees' fight for survival, as told by the charismatic boss and his veteran captain Seamus Coleman.

programme.title: PL STORIES EP15: TIM CAHILL
programme.sub-title: With over 200 appearances for Everton, Tim Cahill established himself as the high-energy midfielder who helped drive the side. For PL Stories this week, Cahill talks through his journey to the Premier League from battling his way to make it as a footballer in his native Australia, leaving home as a teenager and adapting to English life, scoring in a World Cup as well as the difficult decision to leave Goodison Park after 8 years.
programme.desc: With over 200 appearances for Everton, Tim Cahill established himself as the high-energy midfielder who helped drive the side. programme.title: PL STORIES EP12: MARK BOSNICH
programme.sub-title: He was Villa’s penalty saving hero in the 90s and the man tasked by Fergie to replace the legendary Peter Schmeichel at Manchester United. Former Australian number one, Mark Bosnich welcomes PL Stories down under as he reflects on his career in England’s top-flight.

programme.sub-title: Andy Robertson has been integral to the Liverpool team which has won everything in recent years, from the Premier League to the UEFA Champions League. programme.title: PL STORIES EP07: FERGIE v WENGER
programme.sub-title: Widely regarded as the two best managers in Premier League history, Sir Alex Ferguson and Arsene Wenger went toe to toe on the touchline on numerous occasions over the years. But what made their rivalry so fierce? We take a look at the intense clashes, memorable moments on and off the pitch and the monumental impact their duels had on English football's modern era.

programme.sub-title: We sit down with one of the most enigmatic managers of all time, Claudio Ranieri, as he looks back on an incredible career which saw him manage top-flight clubs in Italy, England, Spain and France.

programme.title: THE BIG INTERVIEW EP07: ANDY ROBERTSON
programme.sub-title: Andy Robertson has been integral to the Liverpool team which has won everything in recent years, from the Premier League to the UEFA Champions League. However, his route to the top hasn’t always been straight-forward. Robertson opens up about being released by his boyhood club Celtic, playing and working for Queens Park, Jackie McNamara’s importance to him whilst at Dundee and how he’s adapting to life under new Liverpool boss, Arne Slot.

programme.title: THE BIG INTERVIEW EP04: JOHN BARNES
programme.sub-title: Growing up in a middle-class, military family in Jamaica, John Barnes moved to England as a 12-year-old, where he was later scouted by Watford. A key player in the Liverpool side involved in the inception of the Premier League, Barnes gives his thoughts to The Big Interview on his own stellar career, how the league has changed over the past 32 years and why the number 10 role that he often occupied isn’t as prominent as it used to be.

programme.title: PL STORIES EP20: (Subbed) EMMANUEL ADEBAYOR
programme.sub-title: PL Stories looks into the extraordinary journey of Emmanuel Adebayor, from his prolific Premier League career with Arsenal, Manchester City, Tottenham, and Crystal Palace, to the harrowing experience of surviving the Togo bus attack in 2010. We dive deep into his rise as one of Africa's finest football talents, and how the near-death tragedy profoundly impacted his life, shaping his resilience and outlook beyond the game.
programme.desc: PL Stories looks into the extraordinary journey of Emmanuel Adebayor, from his prolific Premier League career with Arsenal, Manchester City, Tottenham, and Crystal Palace, to the harrowing experience of surviving the Togo bus attack in 2010. programme.title: 1-TO-11 EP20: EMMANUEL ADEBAYOR

programme.title: PL STORIES EP35: AFRICAN ICONS IN THE PREMIER LEAGUE
programme.sub-title: We discover the impact that African players have had on the Premier League. We hear from the key figures as we track the growth of African influence: from the trail blazers in the early 90s; the Champions who have lifted the Premier League trophy; the modern greats such as Mane, Salah and Mahrez who have set new standards for African footballers; right up to the players who are leaving their mark in the Premier League this season, making Africa’s influence greater than ever before.
